article_title: Neuromorphic Computing Crosses the Production Threshold as Sandia Solves Physics on Brain-Inspired Hardware

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: Neuromorphic Computing Crosses the Production Threshold as Sandia Solves Physics on Brain-Inspired Hardware
content_preview.summary: Sandia demonstrates that spiking neural networks can solve physics simulations on Intel Loihi 2, Intel ships the 4nm Loihi 3 with eight million neurons, and UT San Antonio opens the first public neuromorphic computing hub in the United States.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Neuromorphic computing, the discipline of building processors that mimic the electrochemical signaling of biological neurons, has spent more than a decade as a promising but marginal branch of computer architecture. In early 2026, three developments suggest the field is crossing from laboratory curiosity into practical engineering capability. Sandia National Laboratories has [demonstrated](https://phys.org/news/2026-01-nature-good-math.html) that spiking neural networks running on I...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
editor_notes:
Strong Analysis covering three convergent neuromorphic computing milestones. Summary accurate. Word count (1354) appropriate for Analysis. Includes helpful 'What Neuromorphic Computing Is Not' section that maintains editorial balance.

source_verification:
- phys.org: HTTP 403 on manual fetch. Retrieved via Archive.org during automated review. Sandia press coverage of spiking neural network PDE solver.
- newsroom.intel.com: CONFIRMED. Hala Point system specs: 1152 Loihi 2 processors, 1.15B neurons, 128B synapses, 140,544 cores, 2600W max, 20 petaops.
- news.utsa.edu: CONFIRMED. THOR Neuromorphic Commons, Dhireesha Kudithipudi, MATRIX AI Consortium, nation's first open-access neuromorphic hub.
- nature.com, sciencedaily.com (x2): Not independently fetched but corroborated by other sources.
